digest.greeting: Good morning, Jean. Today is Tuesday, July 21, and the day is anchored by Skills & Amazon Connect training planning, Jhara and Mental Health project follow-ups, and a compressed 2:00-3:00 PM block where EPMO TL, the J&J sync, and Legal Change Management overlap. There are 10 calendar blocks, 3 carried-over items, 0 active reminders, and 4 high-priority moves: close the lingering Incident Response and CoE newsletter loops, turn the Skills/Amazon Connect session into a clear training and communications roadmap, resolve the afternoon coverage conflict, and watch LevelPath readiness before the July 24 go-live. Teams collection was rate-limited, so this brief relies on Outlook Calendar, Outlook Email, and GitHub dashboard history.

items.label: Finish the remaining Incident Response work with Bill
items.detail: This is still open across recent dashboard history. Confirm what remains, decide Bill's next step, and close the loop before it drifts further past Monday's portfolio reset.

items.label: Confirm the CoE newsletter owner and status
items.detail: The CoE newsletter follow-up remains unresolved. Confirm who owns the next send and whether anything is needed before the weekly recap cadence continues.

items.label: Follow up with Katie about the missing File Release project
items.detail: The File Release project follow-up was shown Monday and remains unchecked. Use the morning focus block or the portfolio review window to clarify why the project is not showing and what Katie needs next.

items.label: Focus time
items.detail: Best early window to clear the carried-over Incident Response, CoE newsletter, and File Release follow-ups before the meeting stack starts.

items.label: Skills & Amazon Connect Training Planning Session
items.detail: The agenda targets training approach, timeline, communications, change management, and a program-level roadmap. This is today's main planning anchor.

items.label: 1:1 Jhara & Jean
items.detail: Use the portfolio review agenda to surface roadblocks and next steps before Jhara's Legal Change Management sync later in the day.

items.label: Mental Health project exciting meeting
items.detail: Mar's meeting is framed around the impostor-syndrome workstream. Keep the practical next milestone and owner clear.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: A 90-minute protected buffer before the afternoon compression. Good window for the training-roadmap follow-up and LevelPath readiness review.

items.label: EPMO TL Weekly Catch Up
items.detail: This starts the afternoon leadership block and overlaps with two meetings at 2:30 PM. Decide whether this or one of the 2:30 PM meetings needs Jean live.

items.label: bi-weekly sync (J & J)
items.detail: This overlaps with both EPMO TL and Legal Change Management. Janet's agenda covers workload, challenges, wins, and goals.

items.label: Legal Change Management - IT & EPMO Sync
items.detail: Jhara's sync is meant to align EPMO and IT SharePoint around doubts, action items, and clarifications. It conflicts with the J&J sync and the tail of EPMO TL.

items.label: 1:1 Nicolas & Jean
items.detail: Use this to check any automation, owner, or portfolio handoff items that need a single next owner after the afternoon conflicts.

items.label: CSC Portfolio initial review
items.detail: Hassan is the required attendee. Prepare the initial scope, active risks, and next decision needed for the CSC portfolio path.

items.label: Turn Skills and Amazon Connect planning into a clear roadmap
items.detail: Use the 10:00 AM session to leave with draft training milestones, communications sequencing, change-management language, and leadership-ready recommendations for the broader program plan.

items.label: Decide coverage for the 2:30 PM overlap
items.detail: EPMO TL runs into both the J&J sync and Legal Change Management. Decide before the afternoon which conversation needs Jean live and where context or delegation is enough.

items.label: Track LevelPath UAT and SSO readiness before go-live
items.detail: Laura's Asana status says UAT moved to July 20, go-live remains targeted for July 24, and production SSO is still unresolved while LevelPath checks configuration. Confirm whether the SSO issue and training-material review are closing on time.

items.label: Use the Jhara and Mental Health meetings to lock next steps
items.detail: The late-morning sequence moves from Jhara's portfolio roadblocks into Mar's Mental Health project meeting. Keep each conversation tied to a next owner, milestone, or blocker rather than leaving them as open discussion.

items.label: Prepare the CSC Portfolio initial review
items.detail: Before the 4:00 PM meeting with Hassan, define the initial portfolio scope, expected decisions, and the first follow-up owner so the review creates a usable path forward.

items.label: Legal Change Management - IT & EPMO Sync
items.detail: Tomorrow starts with the same EPMO and IT SharePoint alignment thread; carry forward any unresolved clarification from today's overlap.

items.label: 1:1 Thiago & Jean
items.detail: Good follow-up window for Amazon Connect, especially after today's training and communications planning session.

items.label: 1:1 Maria & Jean, with canceled Jean/Sara 1:1 after it
items.detail: The Maria 1:1 is followed by a canceled Sara 1:1 and a 12:00 PM blocked space, creating room to capture morning follow-ups.

items.label: EPMO Team Meeting: Attorney Client Privilege training
items.detail: Chelsea Schinnour is listed as the special guest for attorney-client privilege training, with updates and reminders also on the agenda.

items.label: CoE Biweekly Touchpoint overlaps focus time
items.detail: CoE Biweekly Touchpoint and preparation focus time both run at 4:00 PM, followed by Jean/Sara 1:1 at 4:30 PM.
